Index.title: FITA | Execution without scope surprises
Index.description: Execution contractor for civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation, and coatings. FITA enters according to project scope and, when applicable, according to the client’s engineering and technical requirements.

Footer.description: Civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, and coatings. Partial or integral. Through bid, invitation, or direct award, according to project scope and, when applicable, according to client engineering.

Hero.headline: When scope is clear, projects close well.
Hero.subheadline: FITA enters with what it can actually execute: no inflated promises and no blurred scope. Civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation, and coatings for public and private projects, in partial or broader scopes and, when applicable, according to the client’s engineering and technical requirements.
Hero.panelBullet1: We enter the package that belongs to us, not the one that sounds better
Hero.panelBullet2: Civil, metal-mechanical work, installation, and coatings under one point of contact
Hero.panelBullet3: External specialists when the project requires them, without losing control of the work front
Hero.formalValue: Email for bid packages, invitations, and technical scope

Overview.title: The contractor that does not overstate its scope.
Overview.description1: Many companies enter a bid with a profile larger than what they can sustain in the field. FITA does the opposite: it first defines what it can execute, under what responsibility, and under which project conditions. That reduces surprises on site.
Overview.description2: We work in new construction, maintenance, expansion, and rehabilitation — across civil, metal-mechanical, installation, and coating fronts. We can take a single package or a broader execution scope, according to the real project scope and, when applicable, according to the client’s engineering and technical requirements.

scope.label: what we can execute, not what sounds better

lines.label: one point of contact for civil, steel, installation, and coatings

Institutional.standardsTitle: We do not promise what we cannot deliver
Institutional.standardsDesc: We first define which part of the project belongs to FITA and under what responsibility. That prevents scope from growing on paper and shrinking in the field.
Institutional.sectorsDesc: Civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation, and coatings under a single contract. You do not need to coordinate several subcontractors for what FITA can handle directly.
Institutional.collabTitle: We enter the way the project requires
Institutional.collabDesc: Through bid, invitation, direct award, or technical proposal. With client engineering when it exists, or according to bid scope and technical requirements when that is the governing format.

Model.title: The team the project needs. No more, no less.
Model.subtitle: We build capacity according to the work front, the dominant discipline, and the real contract conditions.
Model.description1: We do not force a fixed structure into every project. The team is built according to the type of work, the size of the front, and what the contract actually requires. That keeps us agile without losing technical control.
Model.description2: When the project requires highly specific specialties — radiography, penetrant testing, soil mechanics, topography — we add the specialist without making the client lose its main point of contact.

Capabilities.title: Four fronts. One accountable contractor.
Capabilities.subtitle: Civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation and erection, and coatings, all under FITA’s direct coordination.

items.title: Civil work
items.description: Foundations, finishes, waterproofing, guardhouses, sports courts, and institutional rehabilitation. We can participate from opening through front delivery.

items.title: Metal-mechanical and structural work
items.description: Fabrication, preparation, erection, qualified welding, piping, and plate work for industrial infrastructure. Supported by documented offshore execution history.

items.title: Installation and erection
items.description: Integration of components and equipment in the field, including electromechanical fronts when they are part of the contracted scope. We coordinate without losing control of the front.

items.title: Anticorrosive coatings
items.description: Sandblasting, surface preparation, and application of protection systems for exposed or industrial infrastructure. Documented experience on offshore platforms.

Projects.title: We have already been in projects like yours.
Projects.subtitle: Offshore with Petrofac, metal-mechanical structures, industrial coatings, and institutional civil work. Fronts already executed and documented.

items.title: Offshore platforms
items.description: Structural panels, pipe defenses, risers, process lines, handrails, and dismantling on Arenque platforms with Petrofac México. Demanding environment, documented scope.

items.title: Metal-mechanical fabrication and erection
items.description: Steel structures, piping assemblies, valves, qualified welding, and structural erection. The kind of work many companies contract badly and end up correcting in the field.

items.title: Coatings and protection
items.description: Sandblasting and anticorrosive systems for platforms, helipads, and industrial infrastructure. Proper surface preparation from the start.

items.title: Maintenance and modifications
items.description: Corrective maintenance, reinforcements, dismantling, lifting, and front conditioning. We enter when the work has already started and the front needs order.

items.title: Institutional civil work
items.description: Domes, laboratories, guardhouses, sports courts, and facility rehabilitation. Projects that were completed and can be verified.

items.title: Adaptable scope
items.description: We do not arrive with only one format. If the project needs a package, we quote the package. If it needs an integral front, we structure it. The client defines the format.

Safety.title: No scope surprises. No avoidable accidents. No half-finished deliveries.
Safety.subtitle: Clients that hire FITA should not have to supervise what is being promised in the field versus what the contract actually says.

items.title: Safety as an entry condition
items.description: Not as a presentation checklist. Safety is built into how the front is organized from day one — access, PPE, lifting procedures, and work risk management.

items.title: What the contract says is what gets delivered
items.description: Each package matches what is contracted, executed, and deliverable. If there is a deviation, it is recorded and resolved — not hidden until final closeout.

items.title: Order on site, always
items.description: Cleanliness, waste segregation, and compliance with project and client requirements. You should not have to chase us to keep the work front presentable.

Methodology.title: Six steps so scope is never a surprise.
Methodology.subtitle: Before starting, the client already knows what FITA will execute, with which resources, and under what responsibility.

steps.title: Opportunity review
steps.description: We analyze the bid, invitation, or requirement to define whether FITA should enter the full project or only part of it. We do not quote what we cannot sustain.

steps.title: Real scope definition
steps.description: We define exactly what FITA executes and what it does not. This is also where we determine whether we work from bid scope, client engineering, or both.

steps.title: Operational planning
steps.description: We organize personnel, sequencing, supplies, and timing according to the real job. The work plan is not a presentation document — it is the guide for the front.

steps.title: Field or workshop execution
steps.description: We execute the contracted scope with the discipline the project requires: civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation, or coatings. The client has one direct point of contact throughout.

steps.title: Control and coordination
steps.description: We monitor scope compliance, safety, quality, and progress. If there is a deviation, the client knows before it becomes a problem.

steps.title: Delivery and continuity
steps.description: We close the executed front with the documentation the client needs. And if there are next phases, FITA already knows the project — there is no learning curve reset.

Procurement.title: Do you have a project that needs a contractor? FITA enters through the channel you use.
Procurement.description: Through public bid, restricted invitation, or direct award. We send technical proposals, quote specific packages, or structure a broader front — according to project scope and, when applicable, according to the client’s bid documents, technical requirements, and engineering.
Procurement.bullet1: Email for formal invitations, bid packages, and technical proposals
Procurement.bullet2: WhatsApp for first contact, interest validation, or scope questions
Procurement.bullet3: Partial or broader pricing according to what the bid defines

Contact.title: Do you have a work front that needs a contractor? Start with the scope.
Contact.subtitle: Tell us what type of project it is, what stage it is in, and what the project needs. We will reply through the same channel you used to contact us.
Contact.name: Ing. Gustavo Bierge
Contact.address: Paseo de los Mexicas 126-D, 16 de Septiembre, Ciudad Madero, Tamaulipas, postal code 89512, Mexico.
Contact.coverage: FITA can participate in public or private projects, in partial or broader scopes, according to project scope and, when applicable, according to the client’s engineering and technical requirements.
Contact.formalValue: Bid invitations, proposal requests, scope package review, and technical submissions
Contact.quickValue: Interest validation, initial questions, or first contact through WhatsApp

Faq.title: The questions clients ask before inviting us.
Faq.subtitle: Direct answers to the objections that usually come up before trusting a work front to a contractor.

items.title: Can FITA handle simultaneous fronts or does it only work on one project at a time?
items.description: It depends on the size of the front. FITA structures its capacity according to the job — if the project allows it, we can keep several fronts active. We define that in the initial scope review so we do not commit beyond what we can sustain.

items.title: Can I invite FITA only for a specific package or do I need to give it the whole project?
items.description: We can enter a single package. You do not need to give us the whole project for us to participate. Civil work, metal-mechanical work, installation, or coatings — we quote the front you need.

items.title: How verifiable is FITA’s experience? Are these only site claims?
items.description: The track record is documented in the company CV with participation records in Petrofac México (Arenque platforms), metal-mechanical fabrication work, and institutional civil work. That file can be shared during supplier review.

items.title: Does FITA work from client engineering or can it work from technical scope defined by the process?
items.description: Both. When the client has defined engineering, we execute according to it. When the scope comes from a bid or technical requirement, we work with what the process defines. We enter according to project scope and, when applicable, according to the client’s engineering and technical requirements.

items.title: How do I start contact if I want to invite FITA to participate in a project?
items.description: For formal invitations, bid packages, or technical proposals, write to cmfitasadecv@gmail.com. To validate interest or ask questions first, use WhatsApp at +52 833 518 1171 with Ing. Gustavo Bierge.
